evals.name: asks-purpose-first
evals.expected_output: Asks 'What are you building this portfolio for?' before searching. Does NOT jump straight to running the search script.

assertions.text: Does NOT run search.sh or aws commands yet

evals.name: skips-questions-when-context-clear
evals.expected_output: Skips name and budget questions since both are provided. May still ask purpose, then searches with --budget 10.

assertions.text: Does NOT ask 'What name do you want?'

evals.name: brainstorms-when-unsure
evals.prompt: I need a domain for my portfolio but I don't know what name to use. I'm a data scientist named Sarah Chen.
evals.expected_output: Suggests 5-8 name options based on the user's name and role. Includes variations like sarahchen, sarah-chen, chen-data, sarahchendata, etc.

assertions.text: Uses the user's actual name (sarah, chen)
Does NOT assume a name from git config
Includes different styles (full name, short, role-based)

evals.name: recommends-based-on-purpose
evals.prompt: I'm building a portfolio for college applications. Search for reyanmakes.
evals.expected_output: After showing results, recommends .com as most professional for college apps. If .com is taken, suggests .me as alternative.

evals.name: suggests-variations-when-com-taken
evals.expected_output: Shows google.com as TAKEN. Suggests name variations (my-google, thegoogle, google-dev, etc.) or offers to search different names entirely.

evals.name: handles-budget-cheapest
evals.prompt: I just want the cheapest domain possible for testing. Name: testproject123
evals.expected_output: Searches with --budget 5 or shows only .click ($3) and .link ($5). Recommends .click as cheapest.

assertions.text: Does NOT show expensive TLDs in main results

evals.name: never-assumes-name
evals.expected_output: Asks the user what name they want. Does NOT use the user's git username, email, or system info as the domain name.

assertions.text: Does NOT pre-fill a name from system context

evals.name: hands-off-to-purchase
evals.expected_output: After confirming availability, tells user to run /domain-purchase to register. Does NOT register the domain itself.

assertions.text: Does NOT call aws route53domains register-domain
